title: About Sai Tarrun Pitta
text: Sai Tarrun Pitta is a Full-Stack Software Engineer with 3+ years of experience designing and delivering scalable backend systems, distributed cloud platforms, and AI-enabled applications. He is skilled in building high-volume payment platforms, cloud infrastructure, RAG retrieval pipelines, and secure production systems, with a strong focus on reliability, performance optimization, and security-first engineering. He is currently available for full-time software engineer roles.

title: Contact Information
text: Sai Tarrun Pitta can be reached at email: saitarrunpitta@gmail.com. LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/saitarrunpitta. GitHub: github.com/saitarrun. Portfolio website: https://saitarrun.dev. Phone: +1 (657) 751-9260.

title: Job Availability
text: Sai Tarrun Pitta is currently available for work and actively seeking full-time software engineer positions. He is open to backend, full-stack, and AI/ML engineering roles. He is based in California and has experience working both in the US and India.

title: Pacific Life - Software Engineer Intern
text: Sai worked at Pacific Life as a Software Engineer Intern from May 2025 to December 2025 in Newport Beach, California. He automated 98% of claims triage workflows by architecting a serverless ML/LLM service using AWS Lambda, Amazon API Gateway, TensorFlow, and LangChain multi-agent orchestration. He reduced claims processing time by 75% (from 3 hours to 45 minutes) and workflow failures by 30% by implementing human-in-the-loop validation checkpoints. He accelerated policy lookup by 98% and boosted claims processor productivity by 50% by engineering a secure RAG pipeline with LLM embeddings, Pinecone semantic search, source-cited responses, and access-controlled retrieval. He built a TypeScript API aggregation layer with Redis distributed caching, reducing dashboard load time from 4 seconds to 2 seconds (50% improvement) and API p99 latency from 300ms to 210ms. He integrated ML inference into an event-driven Kafka pipeline achieving under 100ms latency for risk updates on 5,000+ weekly claims with 99.99% reliability.

title: Accenture - Software Engineer
text: Sai worked at Accenture as a Software Engineer from July 2022 to June 2024 in Hyderabad, India. He scaled a gRPC-based transaction microservice to process 2 million+ daily transactions with 20,000 transactions per second peak throughput and 99.99% uptime using Protocol Buffers, idempotency keys, and Redis distributed locking. He led AWS cloud infrastructure optimization using Amazon EKS auto-scaling and Amazon ElastiCache connection pooling, reducing compute costs by 50% and cloud spending by 35% (saving $15,000 per month). He optimized SQL queries across distributed transaction systems using composite indexes and query refactoring, reducing latency by 60% and database CPU usage by 73%. He designed a reliability architecture with Amazon CloudWatch, Prometheus, and Splunk monitoring pipelines with automated alerts, reducing mean time to recovery (MTTR) by 25 times for business-critical services.

title: Cognizant - Software Engineer
text: Sai worked at Cognizant as a Software Engineer from May 2021 to April 2022 in Hyderabad, India. He improved PostgreSQL query performance and resolved lock contention in Spring Boot services, reducing API latency by 52% (from 2.5 seconds to 1.2 seconds) and lowering p99 latency by 30%. He engineered an ML churn prediction system using scikit-learn and TensorFlow with React dashboards across 50,000 customers, achieving 12% churn reduction and 87% forecast accuracy. He built REST APIs exposing real-time churn predictions for 5,000+ customers, enabling proactive retention operations. He implemented RBAC and input validation, remediating 40+ high-severity vulnerabilities for a clean security audit. He standardized CI/CD with Docker and GitHub Actions, reducing release lead time by 50% and enabling daily deployments.

title: California State University, Fullerton - Master of Science Computer Science
text: Sai is pursuing a Master of Science in Computer Science at California State University, Fullerton from August 2024 to May 2026. He published a peer-reviewed IEEE conference paper titled 'Hardware Trojan Detection with Machine Learning and Power Side-Channels: A Post-Deployment Analysis' at IEEE CNS 2025. His coursework includes Advanced Algorithms, Cloud Computing, System Design, Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, and Distributed Systems.

title: LLM-Powered Knowledge Retrieval Platform
text: Sai built a distributed full-stack LLM-Powered Knowledge Retrieval Platform using LangChain, Pinecone, FastAPI, React, and TypeScript. The platform features semantic search, vector indexing, and NLP-driven query matching. It improved information retrieval accuracy by 25% and reduced user search time by 40%. GitHub: https://github.com/saitarrun/LLM-Powered-Knowledge-Retrieval-Platform

title: Production Portfolio & Personal Website
text: Sai built his own production-grade portfolio and personal website using React, Vite, Tailwind CSS, TypeScript, and deployed on Vercel. It features performance optimization, security-first engineering, responsive design, and comprehensive code quality tooling including TypeScript strict mode, ESLint security rules, Prettier formatting, and git pre-commit and pre-push hooks. GitHub: https://github.com/saitarrun/SoftwareEngineer_Portfolio

title: Event-Driven E-Commerce Modernization
text: Sai built an event-driven e-commerce modernization project using Node.js, Kafka, PostgreSQL, Docker, and Kubernetes. He architected a monolith-to-microservices migration using the Strangler pattern, REST APIs, and event-driven design. This enables zero-downtime canary deployments, service-level isolation, and scalable order, payment, and inventory workflows for high-traffic e-commerce operations. GitHub: https://github.com/saitarrun/Event-Driven-Architecture

title: Brain Tumor Detection Pipeline
text: Sai built an end-to-end brain tumor detection pipeline using TensorFlow, Apache Spark, GCP, and OpenCV. This is a distributed ML pipeline for MRI scan classification that leverages Spark for big data processing and cloud-native inference on Google Cloud Platform to achieve clinical-grade tumor detection accuracy. GitHub: https://github.com/saitarrun/Brain-Tumor-Prediction-Using-Machine-Learning-and-Big-Data

text: Sai's backend and API skills include Spring Boot, Node.js, FastAPI, Django, Express, gRPC, GraphQL, REST APIs, and Microservices architecture. He has experience building high-throughput distributed services.

text: Sai's data and caching skills include Redis, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, DynamoDB, Elasticsearch, Kafka, and RabbitMQ. He has hands-on experience with both SQL and NoSQL databases at scale.
